title: frangula microphylla
intro: Frangula microphylla, also known as the small-leaf buckthorn or alder buckthorn, is a type of shrub native to North America. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation due to its anthraquinone content. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and boost the immune system. Frangula microphylla can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
